REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT FRANCISCO ALDANA DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. AS AN INITIAL MATTER, ALLEGATIONS REGARDING A FORGED WILL ARE INCORPORATED INTO EACH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE MATTER OF THE VALIDITY OF THE WILL CANNOT BE PROPERLY ADDRESSED IN THIS PROCEEDING AND SHALL BE ADDRESSED IN THE PENDING PROBATE CASE. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PROPERLY ALLEGE FACTS, NOT CONCLUSIONS (SEE COMPLAINT, PARAGRAPH 6), ESTABLISHING THAT JESUS AND/OR OFELIA ARE SUCCESSORS IN INTEREST TO EACH OF THE TWO DECEDENTS, JOSE AND CARLOS, ESTATES. DEMURRER IS FURTHER SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE FACTS IN SUPPORT OF EACH ELEMENT OF CAUSES OF ACTION FOR MISREPRESENTATION AND CONCEALMENT AS TO THE MOVING DEFENDANT, INCLUDING BUT NO LIMITED TO A DUTY TO DISCLOSE, REASONABLE RELIANCE AND DETRIMENT CAUSED BY SUCH REASONABLE RELIANCE. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE TO DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL AND/OR SELF-REPRESENTED DEFENDANT(S) SET FOR JAN-28-2020 IN DEPARTMENT 610 AT 10:30 AM FOR FAILURE TO FILE AND SERVE ORDER RE DEMURRER PURSUANT TO THE JUL-23-2019 RULING. THE DEC-11-2019 CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS OFF CALENDAR. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT ARTURO RODRIGUEZ MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND IS DENIED. ACCORDING TO THE NOTICE OF MOTION, THE SOLE BASIS FOR RELIEF IS CCP 473. CCP 473 REQUIRES THAT REQUEST FOR RELIEF SHALL BE MADE WITHIN "REASONABLE TIME, IN NO CASE EXCEEDING SIX MONTHS." THIS MOTION WAS FILED ON NOVEMBER 27, 2019. FURTHER, NOTICE OF MOTION IS SEEKING TO VACATE A DEFAULT ENTERED ON "APRIL 10, 2019." HOWEVER, DEFENDANT RODRIGUEZ' DEFAULT WAS ENTERED ON FEBRUARY 26, 2019, AND BASED ON A LETTER ATTACHED TO ALDANA'S DECLARATION, ALDANA KNEW OF THIS DEFAULT AS OF MARCH 7, 2019 AT THE LATEST. TO THE EXTENT THE MOTION IS SEEKING EQUITABLE RELIEF DISCUSSED IN THE POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, SUCH RELIEF IS NOT PROPERLY BEFORE THE COURT AS IT IS NOT SPECIFIED IN THE NOTICE OF MOTION. HOWEVER, THE COURT WILL NOTE THAT THE BASIS FOR SUCH RELIEF IS NOT SUPPORTED BY FACTS. RODRIGUEZ WAS DEFAULTED ON FEBRUARY 26, 2019. ALDANA CLAIMS THAT HE WAS "UNDER THE IMPRESSION THAT DURING HIS MARCH 6, 2019 TELEPHONE CONVERSATION WITH ATTORNEY MILLER AN EXTENSION WAS GRACIOUSLY GRANTED." ON MARCH 6, 2019 THERE WAS NO EXTENSION TO HAVE BEEN GRANTED AS DEFAULT HAD ALREADY BEEN ENTERED. FURTHERMORE, NO DETAILS ARE PROVIDED AS TO THE LENGTH OF SUCH PURPORTED EXTENSION. THE COURT FURTHER NOTES THAT COUNSEL AGAIN FAILED TO COMPLY WITH SFLR 2.7B (SEE MAY 24, 2019 MINUTE ORDER) AND FAILED TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS. FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH SFLR IN THE FUTURE WILL RESULT IN MOTION BEING TAKEN OFF CALENDAR. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC3.1312(B). JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: N. WONG, NOT REPORTED (D501)

Off Calendar Feb-06-2020 Continued To Feb-27-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: PLAINTIFFs OFELIA MUNGUIA, JESUS MUNGUIA SANCHEZ, and CARLOS AUSEJO's Motion For Order To Disqualify Defendant Francisco Aldana From Representing Defendant Arturo Rodriguez, Jerry Velasco And Any Other Defendant is CONTINUED to February 27, 2020. The parties are ordered to comply with Local Rule 2.7(B) by providing this Court with courtesy copies of the Opposition and Reply by February 13, 2020 with an updated cover page reflecting the new hearing date. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-24-2020 Continued To Apr-14-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Dec-27-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Francisco Aldana, Esq., 619-236-8355, appearing for Arturo Rodriguez, Defendant and moving party.

Argued. Mr. Aldana requested a continuance to January 28, 2020 to file an amendment as to the date of the default. The Court stated that a new motion should be filed.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ARTURO RODRIGUEZ MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is DENIED. According to the Notice of Motion, the sole basis for relief is CCP 473. CCP 473 requires that request for relief shall be made within "reasonable time, in no case exceeding six months." This motion was filed on November 27, 2019. Further, Notice of Motion is seeking to vacate a default entered on "April 10, 2019." However, Defendant Rodriguez' default was entered on February 26, 2019, and based on a letter attached to Aldana's declaration, Aldana knew of this default as of March 7, 2019 at the latest.
To the extent the motion is seeking equitable relief discussed in the Points and Authorities, such relief is not properly before the Court as it is not specified in the Notice of Motion. However, the Court will note that the basis for such relief is not supported by facts. Rodriguez was defaulted on February 26, 2019. Aldana claims that he was "under the impression that during his March 6, 2019 telephone conversation with Attorney Miller an extension was graciously granted." On March 6, 2019 there was no extension to have been granted as default had already been entered. Furthermore, no details are provided as to the length of such purported extension. The Court further notes that counsel again failed to comply with SFLR 2.7B (see May 24, 2019 Minute Order) and failed to provide courtesy copies of the moving papers. Failure to comply with SFLR in the future will result in motion being taken off calendar. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside
Off Calendar Jul-23-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT ARTURO RODRIGUEZ Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside is OFF CALENDAR, no valid proof of service on file. The complaint and the demurrer filed by Franciso Javier Aldana on April 22, 2019 indicate that Fransico Janiver Aldana is a defendant. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-23-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT FRANCISCO ALDANA DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend. As an initial matter, allegations regarding a forged will are incorporated into each cause of action. The matter of the validity of the will cannot be properly addressed in this proceeding and shall be addressed in the pending probate case. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to properly allege facts, not conclusions (See Complaint, paragraph 6), establishing that Jesus and/or Ofelia are successors in interest to each of the two decedents, Jose and Carlos, estates. Demurrer is further sustained with leave to amend to allege facts in support of each element of causes of action for misrepresentation and concealment as to the moving defendant, including but no limited to a duty to disclose, reasonable reliance and detriment caused by such reasonable reliance. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
